evals.expected_output: A feature file with: Feature description (two-factor authentication), Background (given a user exists in the database), and at least 4 scenarios: (1) successful auth with correct password and card; (2) fail with wrong password; (3) fail with correct password but wrong card UID; (4) fail with unknown username. Each scenario should have Given/When/Then steps. The feature file should be executable (step definitions stubs or pytest-bdd compatible).

assertions.text: Feature file has valid Gherkin syntax (Feature, Scenario, Given/When/Then)
Covers at least 4 scenarios including all failure paths
The scenarios accurately reflect the function's logic (3 separate failure conditions)
Pytest-bdd stub functions are provided or the steps are clearly structured for step definition

evals.prompt:
Backtrack this SQL procedure to BDD feature files:
```sql
CREATE PROCEDURE usp_GetBestPrice @ProductID INT AS
BEGIN
  SELECT TOP 1 Price FROM PriceHistory
  WHERE ProductID = @ProductID
  AND EffectiveDate <= GETDATE()
  ORDER BY EffectiveDate DESC
END
```
evals.expected_output: Feature file for price retrieval with scenarios: (1) product has historical prices — returns most recent price effective before today; (2) product has no prices — returns NULL or empty result set; (3) product has future-dated prices — those are excluded (EffectiveDate > GETDATE()); (4) multiple prices on same date (edge case). Background: Given a product exists with ID @ProductID.

assertions.text: Correctly captures the 'effective date <= today' business rule as a scenario
Includes a scenario for future-dated prices being excluded
